title: haloragis exalata
intro: Haloragis exalata, also known as tall ragwort or New Zealand teazle, is a flowering plant native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Haloragis exalata include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Haloragis exalata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
